void add_eas_records(const std::string& table_name,
                     const std::vector<std::tuple<std::string, std::string, int, std::string, std::optional<std::string>, double, std::string>>& records) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  for (const auto& record_tuple : records) {
    if (table_name == "Student") {
      Student student;
      student.SNo = std::get<0>(record_tuple);
      student.SName = std::get<1>(record_tuple);
      student.Sex = std::get<2>(record_tuple);
      student.Birthday = sqlgen::DateTime::from_string(std::get<3>(record_tuple));
      student.Major = std::get<4>(record_tuple).empty() ? std::nullopt : std::make_optional(std::stoul(std::get<4>(record_tuple)));
      student.Scorses = std::get<5>(record_tuple);
      student.Remark = std::get<6>(record_tuple);
      conn->insert(student);
    } else if (table_name == "Course") {
      Course course;
      course.CName = std::get<0>(record_tuple);
      course.Term = std::get<1>(record_tuple);
      course.CTime = std::get<2>(record_tuple);
      course.Credit = std::get<3>(record_tuple);
      conn->insert(course);
    } else if (table_name == "Score") {
      Score score;
      score.SNo = std::get<0>(record_tuple);
      score.CID = std::get<1>(record_tuple);
      score.Score = std::get<2>(record_tuple);
      conn->insert(score);
    } else if (table_name == "Major") {
      Major major;
      major.MID = std::get<0>(record_tuple);
      major.MName = std::get<1>(record_tuple);
      conn->insert(major);
    } else {
      throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table: " + table_name);
    }
  }
}

template <const char* table_name>
void modify_record_impl(const std::string& table_name,
                        const typename TableDetails<table_name>::PrimaryKeyType& record_id,
                        const std::string& col_name,
                        const std::optional<std::string>& new_value) {
  using StructType = typename TableDetails<table_name>::StructType;
  using PrimaryKeyType = typename TableDetails<table_name>::PrimaryKeyType;
  const std::string& primary_key_col_name = TableDetails<table_name>::primary_key_col_name;

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  auto query = conn->update<StructType>()
      .where(primary_key_col_name == record_id)
      .set(col_name, new_value);

  query.execute();
}

void modify_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& table_name,
                              const std::string& record_id,
                              const std::string& col_name,
                              const std::optional<std::string>& new_value) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  if (table_name == "Student") {
    Student student;
    auto res = conn->select_from<Student>().where("SNo = ?").param(record_id).execute();
    if (!res) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Student not found: " + record_id);
    }
    student = res.value().at(0);
    // Update the column
    if (col_name == "SNo") {
      student.SNo = new_value.value();
    } else if (col_name == "SName") {
      student.SName = new_value.value();
    } else if (col_name == "Sex") {
      student.Sex = std::stoi(new_value.value());
    } else if (col_name == "Birthday") {
      student.Birthday = sqlgen::DateTime::from_string(new_value.value());
    } else if (col_name == "Major") {
      student.Major = std::stoul(new_value.value());
    } else if (col_name == "Scorses") {
      student.Scorses = std::stod(new_value.value());
    } else if (col_name == "Remark") {
      student.Remark = new_value.value();
    } else {
      throw std::runtime_error("Invalid column name: " + col_name);
    }
    // Update the record
    conn->update(student).where("SNo = ?").param(student.SNo).execute();
  } else if (table_name == "Course") {
    Course course;
    auto res = conn->select_from<Course>().where("CID = ?").param(std::stoi(record_id)).execute();
    if (!res) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Course not found: " + record_id);
    }
    course = res.value().at(0);
    if (col_name == "CID") {
      // Cannot modify primary key (auto-incr)
      throw std::runtime_error("Cannot modify primary key CID");
    } else if (col_name == "CName") {
      course.CName = new_value.value();
    } else if (col_name == "Term") {
      course.Term = std::stoi(new_value.value());
    } else if (col_name == "CTime") {
      course.CTime = std::stoi(new_value.value());
    } else if (col_name == "Credit") {
      course.Credit = std::stoi(new_value.value());
    } else {
      throw std::runtime_error("Invalid column name: " + col_name);
    }
    conn->update(course).where("CID = ?").param(course.CID).execute();
  } else if (table_name == "Score") {
    Score score;
    auto res = conn->select_from<Score>().where("ScID = ?").param(std::stoi(record_id)).execute();
    if (!res) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Score not found: " + record_id);
    }
    score = res.value().at(0);
    if (col_name == "ScID") {
      throw std::runtime_error("Cannot modify primary key ScID");
    } else if (col_name == "SNo") {
      score.SNo = new_value.value();
    } else if (col_name == "CID") {
      score.CID = std::stoi(new_value.value());
    } else if (col_name == "Score") {
      score.Score = std::stod(new_value.value());
    } else {
      throw std::runtime_error("Invalid column name: " + col_name);
    }
    conn->update(score).where("ScID = ?").param(score.ScID).execute();
  } else if (table_name == "Major") {
    Major major;
    auto res = conn->select_from<Major>().where("MID = ?").param(std::stoi(record_id)).execute();
    if (!res) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Major not found: " + record_id);
    }
    major = res.value().at(0);
    if (col_name == "MID") {
      throw std::runtime_error("Cannot modify primary key MID");
    } else if (col_name == "MName") {
      major.MName = new_value.value();
    } else {
      throw std::runtime_error("Invalid column name: " + col_name);
    }
    conn->update(major).where("MID = ?").param(major.MID).execute();
  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table: " + table_name);
  }
}

if (commit_res) {

} else {
  return 1; // or whatever error code is appropriate

However, the problem says to return the operation result of the commit. So maybe the function should return the number of rows deleted, but the problem's instruction is to return the commit's result. This is a bit ambiguous.

Looking back at the problem statement: "return operation result of commit". So the commit's result is what's returned. So the function should return 0 if the commit was successful, and non-zero otherwise. So regardless of how many rows were deleted, the return value is based on the commit's success.

But in practice, the code would need to handle the transaction. So even if the delete fails, the transaction is rolled back. But the problem says to use a transaction to ensure safety. So the code should start a transaction, perform the delete, then commit. If any step fails, rollback.

So putting it all together:

The code would:

- Connect to the database.
- Begin transaction.
- Execute the delete query.
- If the delete is successful, commit. If commit is successful, return 0. Else, return error code.
- If any step before commit fails, rollback and return error.

But how to handle the delete's result. For example, if the delete query itself fails (e.g., syntax error), then the transaction is still active. So after catching an error during the delete, we need to rollback.

So the code structure would be:

try {
  conn->begin_transaction();
  auto query = delete_from<User>().where(last_login < cutoff_date);
  auto res = conn->execute(query);
  if (!res) {

    conn->rollback();

  auto commit_res = conn->commit();
  if (!commit_res) {

  }
  return 0;

  return 1;

But in the code, the execute() returns a Result<Nothing>, so checking if res is error.

So the code would need to handle both the execute and commit steps.

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace delete_inactive_users {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> last_login;
  std::string status = "active";
};

int delete_inactive_users() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::ConnectionPool::make("test_db.sqlite").value();
    const auto cutoff_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 1, 1};
    
    conn->begin_transaction();
    
    auto query = sqlgen::delete_from<User>().where([](const auto& u) {
      return u.last_login < sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 1, 1};
    });
    
    auto res = conn->execute(query);
    if (!res) {
      conn->rollback();
      return 1; // error executing delete
    }
    
    auto commit_res = conn->commit();
    if (!commit_res) {
      return 2; // error committing
    }
    
    return 0; // success
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Log the exception if needed
    return 3; // general error
  }
}

} // namespace delete_inactive_users

<think>Okay, I need to write a function called design_eas_database using the sqlgen library to create three tables in an SQLite database named test_db.sqlite. Let's start by understanding the requirements.

First, the Student table. The fields are SNo (varchar(10), primary key, not null), SName (varchar(30), not null), Sex (int, default 1), Birthday (datetime), Major (int), Scorses (float, default 0), and Remark (text). Wait, the Scorses field in the table description is named "Scorses" but in the problem statement, maybe that's a typo? Because in the Score table, there's a Score field. But according to the given table, the Student table has Scorses. So I'll have to use that name.

Next, the Course table. Fields are CID (int, auto-increment primary key), CName (varchar(30), not null), Term (int, default 1), CTime (int), Credit (float, default 0). The CID is an auto-incrementing integer, so in SQLite, that would be INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT.

Then the Score table. It has SNo (foreign key to Student.SNo), CID (foreign key to Course.CID), Score (float). The primary key is a composite of SNo and CID. Also, both foreign keys need to reference their respective tables.

Now, using the sqlgen library. The user provided a code skeleton with structs for Student, Course, Score, and the function design_eas_database. I need to define these structs with appropriate attributes and then create the tables in the database.

For each struct, I should use sqlgen's Table and Column classes. For example, the Student struct will have columns defined with their types and constraints. Let's think about each struct:

Student struct:
- SNo: primary key, varchar(10), not null.
- SName: varchar(30), not null.
- Sex: int, default 1.
- Birthday: datetime, nullable.
- Major: int, nullable.
- Scorses: float, default 0.
- Remark: text, nullable.

In sqlgen, the Column type can be specified. For example, sqlgen::VarChar<10> for SNo. The primary key is set with .primary(), and default values with .default(...).

Course struct:
- CID: integer primary key, auto-increment. In sqlgen, using sqlgen::AutoIncr<int> and marking it as primary.
- CName: varchar(30), not null.
- Term: int, default 1.
- CTime: int, nullable.
- Credit: float, default 0.

Score struct:
- SNo: foreign key to Student.SNo, varchar(10), not null.
- CID: foreign key to Course.CID, int, not null.

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <chrono>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create User table
  auto user_table = sqlgen::create_table_t<User>();
  conn->execute(user_table.to_sql());

  // Create Post table
  auto post_table = sqlgen::create_table_t<Post>();
  conn->execute(post_table.to_sql());

  // Define date range
  auto start = sqlgen::make_time(2025, 12, 1);
  auto end = sqlgen::make_time(2025, 12, 31);

  // Build and execute the query
  auto query = conn->select_from<User>()
      .join<Post, User::user_id == Post::user_id>()
      .where(Post::created_at >= start && Post::created_at <= end)
      .select(User::user_id, User::username)
      .distinct()
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<ActiveUser> result;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          result.emplace_back(
              .user_id = res.get<User::user_id>(),
              .username = res.get<User::username>());
        }
        return result;
      });

  return query.value();
}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts
</answer>

#include <optional>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create User table
  auto user_table = sqlgen::create_table_t<User>();
  conn->execute(user_table.to_sql());

  // Create Post table
  auto post_table = sqlgen::create_table_t<Post>();
  conn->execute(post_table.to_sql());

  // Define date range
  auto start = sqlgen::make_time(2025, 12, 1);
  auto end = sqlgen::make_time(2025, 12, 31);

  // Build and execute the query
  auto query = conn->select_from<User>()
      .join<Post, User::user_id == Post::user_id>()
      .where(Post::created_at >= start && Post::created_at <= end)
      .select(User::user_id, User::username)
      .distinct()
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<ActiveUser> result;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          result.emplace_back(
              .user_id = res.get<User::user_id>(),
              .username = res.get<User::username>());
        }

}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "query_course_enrollment_stats.hpp"

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    conn.create_table<Course>();
    conn.create_table<Enrollment>();

    const auto query = R"(
        SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) as student_count, AVG(e.grade) as avg_grade
        FROM Course c
        LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id
        GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;
    )";

    const auto res = conn.execute(query);

    std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
    for (const auto& row : res) {
        CourseStats cs;
        cs.title = row["title"].as<std::string>();
        cs.student_count = row["student_count"].as<int>();
        cs.avg_grade = row["avg_grade"]
            .transform([](const auto& val) { return val.as<double>(); }));
        stats.push_back(std::move(cs));
    }
    return stats;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats
```</answer>

}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics {

struct PageVisit {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> visit_id;
  std::string url;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> visit_time;
};

struct DailyTraffic {
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

struct DailyTrafficView {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "DailyTrafficView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

std::vector<DailyTraffic> query_daily_traffic_metrics() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto query = R"(
      SELECT strftime('%Y-%m-%d', visit_time) as visit_date, 
             COUNT(*) as total_visits, 
             COUNT(DISTINCT url) as unique_pages
      FROM PageVisit
      GROUP BY visit_date
      ORDER BY visit_date;
    )";
    return conn->read<std::vector<DailyTraffic>>(query);
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions (e.g., database connection issues)
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics
```</answer>

  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "query_search_books_by_criteria.hpp"

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& _filters) {
  auto db = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

  const auto title_col = sqlgen::col<Book, Title>();
  const auto year_col = sqlgen::col<Book, Year>();
  const auto genre_col = sqlgen::col<Book, Genre>();
  const auto price_col = sqlgen::col<Book, Price>();

  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>(db);

  auto condition = sqlgen::make_condition(true);

  if (_filters.title_keyword) {
    const auto like_pattern = "%" + *_filters.title_keyword + "%";
    const auto title_condition = sqlgen::make_condition(title_col.like(like_pattern));
    condition = condition && title_condition;
  }

  if (_filters.min_year) {
    const auto min_year_condition = sqlgen::make_condition(year_col >= *_filters.min_year);
    condition = condition && min_year_condition;
  }

  if (_filters.genre) {
    const auto genre_condition = sqlgen::make_condition(genre_col == *_filters.genre);
    condition = condition && genre_condition;
  }

  if (_filters.max_price) {
    const auto max_price_condition = sqlgen::make_condition(price_col <= *_filters.max_price);
    condition = condition && max_price_condition;
  }

  query.where(condition);

  return query.read();
}

}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/Connection.hpp"

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

bool migration_add_column() {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto session = conn->session();

    // Retrieve existing column names.
    const auto pragma_result = session->execute("PRAGMA table_info(User);");
    std::vector<std::string> column_names;
    for (const auto& row : pragma_result) {
      column_names.emplace_back(row[1]);
    }

    // Add 'phone_number' column if it doesn't exist.
    if (std::find(column_names.begin(), column_names.end(), "phone_number") ==
          column_names.end()) {
      session->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20);");
    }

    // Add 'is_active' column if it doesn't exist.
    if (std::find(column_names.begin(), column_names.end(), "is_active") ==
          column_names.end()) {
      session->execute(
          "ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1;");
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace schema_migration_add_column
```</answer>

} catch (const std::exception& e) {

Wait, but what if the connection throws an exception during the transaction? So we need to wrap the transaction in a try-catch block. If any exception occurs, rollback.

So the code should be wrapped in a try block, and catch any exceptions, then rollback.

So the code would look like:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <rfl.hpp>

namespace transaction_transfer_funds {

bool transfer_funds(int from_account_id, int to_account_id, double amount) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  try {
    conn.begin_transaction();

    // Deduct from sender
    auto stmt = conn.prepare_statement(
        "UPDATE Account SET balance = balance - ? WHERE account_id = ? AND balance >= ?");
    stmt.bind(1, amount);
    stmt.bind(2, from_account_id);
    stmt.bind(3, amount);
    const auto rows_deducted = stmt.execute();
    if (rows_deducted != 1) {
      conn.rollback();
      return false;
    }

    // Add to receiver
    auto stmt2 = conn.prepare_statement(
        "UPDATE Account SET balance = balance + ? WHERE account_id = ?");
    stmt2.bind(1, amount);
    stmt2.bind(2, to_account_id);
    const auto rows_added = stmt2.execute();
    if (rows_added != 1) {
      conn.rollback();
      return false;
    }

    conn.commit();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    conn.rollback();
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace transaction_transfer_funds

#endif

// update_eas_table.hpp

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

void update_eas_table() {
  design_eas_database_with_major();

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  // 1. Add Grade column to Score table
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Score ADD COLUMN Grade CHAR(1) NOT NULL;").value();

  // 2. Modify Age column in Student table
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Student MODIFY Age INT NULL;").value();

  // 3. Drop Major table
  conn.execute("DROP TABLE Major;").value();
}

} // namespace update_eas_table

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "upsert_user_profile.hpp"

namespace upsert_user_profile {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

bool upsert_user_profile(const UserProfile& _user) {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto insert_or_replace_stmt =
        conn->prepare_statement(
            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO UserProfile (user_id, bio, avatar_url) "
            "VALUES (?, ?, ?);");
    insert_or_replace_stmt->execute(_user.user_id, _user.bio, _user.avatar_url);
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_user_profile
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace right_join_departments_projects {

std::vector<DepProj> right_join_departments_projects() {

            .project_name = _p.name.empty() ? std::nullopt : _p.name

}

}  // namespace right_join_departments_projects
output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace right_join_departments_projects {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Project {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct DepProj {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::string project_name;
};

std::vector<DepProj> right_join_departments_projects() {
  using namespace sqlgen::ranges::views;

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = conn->begin_transaction();

  auto query = session->selectFrom<Project>()
      .join<Department, sqlgen::JoinType::right>(
          [&](const auto& _p, const auto& _d) {
            return _p.department_id == _d.department_id;
          }
      )
      .select([](const auto& _p, const auto& _d) -> DepProj {
        return DepProj{
            .department_name = _d.name,
            .project_name = _p.name.empty() ? std::nullopt : _p.name
        };
      });

  return query.value_or(std::vector<DepProj>());
}
}  // namespace right_join_departments_projects
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <type_traits>

#include "analyze_temporal_data.hpp"

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

struct Event {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data(
    const std::vector<Event>& _events) {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  conn.insert(_events);

  const auto query = conn.select_from<Event>()
      .fields(
          Event::event_id,
          Event::event_name,
          Event::start_time.day(),
          Event::start_time.month(),
          Event::start_time.year(),
          Event::start_time.hour(),
          Event::start_time.minute(),
          Event::start_time.second(),
          Event::start_time.weekday(),
          Event::start_time.unixepoch(),
          (Event::end_time - Event::start_time).days()
      )
      .where(Event::end_time.is_not_null());

  using ResultRow = sqlgen::ResultRow<
      rfl::Tuple<int, std::string, int, int, int, int, int, int, int, int64_t, std::optional<double>>>;
  const auto results = conn.execute(_query.str()).transform(
      [&](auto&& _res) -> std::vector<ResultRow> {
        return _res.transform([](auto&& _it) -> ResultRow {
          return ResultRow::make(
              _it.get<int>(0),
              _it.get<std::string>(1),
              _it.get<int>(2),
              _it.get<int>(3),
              _it.get<int>(4),
              _it.get<int>(5),
              _it.get<int>(6),
              _it.get<int>(7),
              _it.get<int>(8),
              _it.get<int64_t>(9),
              _it.get<std::optional<double>>(10));
        });
      });

  std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyses;
  if (!results) {
    return analyses;
  }

  for (const auto& _row : results.value()) {
    analyses.emplace_back(
        .event_id = _row[0],
        .event_name = _row[1],
        .start_day = _row[2],
        .start_month = _row[3],
        .start_year = _row[4],
        .start_hour = _row[5],
        .start_minute = _row[6],
        .start_second = _row[7],
        .start_weekday = _row[8],
        .start_epoch = _row[9],
        .duration_days = _row[10]);
  }

  return analyses;
}

}  // namespace analyze_temporal_data
```</answer>

But how to do that? Let's think.

The function should:

- Connect to the SQLite database "test_db.sqlite".
- Execute a SELECT query on the Product table with the mathematical operations.
- Fetch the results and map them to the MathAnalysis struct.
- Return the vector of MathAnalysis.

So the code would look something like this:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct MathAnalysis {
  // fields as per the reference SQL
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate/100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<MathAnalysis> result;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    MathAnalysis ma;
    ma.product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>();
    ma.name = row["name"].as<std::string>();
    ma.abs_price = row["abs_price"].as<double>();
    ma.ceil_price = row["ceil_price"].as<double>();
    ma.floor_price = row["floor_price"].as<double>();
    ma.rounded_price = row["rounded_price"].as<double>();
    ma.exp_discount = row["exp_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.ln_price = row["ln_price"].as<double>();
    ma.log2_quantity = row["log2_quantity"].as<double>();
    ma.sin_discount = row["sin_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.cos_discount = row["cos_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.tan_discount = row["tan_discount"].as<double>();
    ma.sqrt_quantity = row["sqrt_quantity"].as<double>();
    result.push_back(ma);
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
};

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate / 100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto result = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyses;
  for (const auto& row : result) {
    MathAnalysis analysis;
    analysis.product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>();
    analysis.name = row["name"].as<std::string>();
    analysis.abs_price = row["abs_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.ceil_price = row["ceil_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.floor_price = row["floor_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.rounded_price = row["rounded_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.exp_discount = row["exp_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.ln_price = row["ln_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.log2_quantity = row["log2_quantity"].as<double>();
    analysis.sin_discount = row["sin_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.cos_discount = row["cos_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.tan_discount = row["tan_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.sqrt_quantity = row["sqrt_quantity"].as<double>();
    analyses.push_back(analysis);
  }
  return analyses;
}
}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "trim_and_length_validation.hpp"

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<UserProfile>(
      UserProfile::user_id,
      UserProfile::name.trim().as("trimmed_name"),
      UserProfile::email.trim().as("trimmed_email"),
      UserProfile::name.trim().length().as("name_length"),
      UserProfile::email.trim().length().as("email_length")
  );

  return conn->template read<std::vector<ValidatedProfile>>(query);
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```</answer>

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

// ... structs ...

std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>(':memory:');

    // Create the OrderItem table
    conn->execute(R"(
      CREATE TABLE OrderItem (
        item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
        order_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
        product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
        unit_price REAL NOT NULL,
        quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
        discount_rate REAL,
        tax_rate REAL
      );
    )");

      SELECT 
        item_id,
        order_id,
        product_id,
        unit_price,
        quantity,
        discount_rate,
        tax_rate,
        (unit_price * quantity) as gross_total,
        (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0)/100)) as discounted_total,
        (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0)/100) * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate, 0)/100)) as final_total,
        (unit_price * quantity / quantity) as unit_cost,
        (quantity % 10) as quantity_group,
        (unit_price * 0.3) as estimated_profit
      FROM OrderItem;
    )";

    const auto result = conn->execute(query);

    if (!result) {
      return {};
    }

    // Map the result rows to OrderMetrics
    std::vector<OrderMetrics> metrics;
    for (const auto& row : *result) {
      OrderMetrics metric{
          .item_id = row["item_id"].as<int>(),
          .order_id = row["order_id"].as<int>(),

          .final_total = row["final_total"].as<double>(),
          .unit_cost = row["unit_cost"].as<double>(),
          .quantity_group = row["quantity_group"].as<int>(),
          .estimated_profit = row["estimated_profit"].as<double>()};
      metrics.push_back(std::move(metric));
    }

    return metrics;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions (log, etc.)
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

#endif

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace join_orders_customers_payments {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  int customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
};

struct Payment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> payment_id;
  int order_id;
  double amount;
};

struct Consolidated {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer_name;
  double amount;
};

std::vector<Consolidated> join_orders_customers_payments() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_conn("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = sqlgen::Session<sqlgen::SQLiteConn>::make(conn);

  const auto query = session->from<Customer>()
      .join<Order, Customer.customer_id == Order.customer_id>()
      .join<Payment, Order.order_id == Payment.order_id>()
      .select(Order.order_id, Customer.name.as("customer_name"), Payment.amount)
      .order_by(Payment.amount.desc())
      .to_sql();

  return session->execute<Consolidated>(query);
}
}  // namespace join_orders_customers_payments
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Replace "foo" with "bar" in all notes
  conn->execute("UPDATE Notes SET content = REPLACE(content, 'foo', 'bar')");

  // Fetch updated notes
  const auto query = "SELECT note_id, content FROM Notes";
  const auto notes = conn->template read<std::vector<Note>>(query);

  // Convert to UpdatedNote format
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    UpdatedNote updated_note{
        .note_id = note.note_id,
        .updated_content = note.content};
    result.push_back(std::move(updated_note));
  }
  return result;
}
}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace cascade_delete_demo {

struct Parent {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> parent_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Child {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> child_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Parent, "parent_id"> parent_id;
  std::string note;
};

bool cascade_delete_demo() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    conn->execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");  // Enable foreign key support

    // Create tables
    conn->create_table<Parent>();
    conn->create_table<Child>());

    // Insert parent
    Parent parent{1, "Test Parent"};
    conn->insert(parent);

    // Insert child
    Child child{1, 1, "Test Note"};
    conn->insert(child);

    // Delete parent
    conn->execute("DELETE FROM Parent WHERE parent_id = 1;");

    // Check if child is deleted
    auto result = conn->execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Child WHERE parent_id = 1;");
    return result.begin()->at(0).as<int>() == 0;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // In case of any error, return false
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace cascade_delete_demo

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "column_is_null_checks.hpp"

namespace column_is_null_checks {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

struct Result {
  int profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
};

std::vector<Result> column_is_null_checks() {
  const auto make_result = [](const auto& _row) -> Result {
    return {._profile_id = _row.profile_id, ._display_name = _row.display_name};
  };

  return sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite")
      .transform([](auto&& _conn) {
        return _conn.selectFrom<Profile>(Profile::profile_id, Profile::display_name)
            .where(Profile::bio.is_null() || Profile::avatar_url.is_null())
            .transform([](auto&& _res) {
              return rfl::apply(
                  [](auto... _ms) {
                    return std::vector<Result>({make_result(_m)...});
                  },
                  _res);
            });
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<Result>());
}
}  // namespace column_is_null_checks
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace select_from_read_to_vector {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  int pages;
};

std::vector<Book> select_from_read_to_vector() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("books.db").value();
  const auto query =
      sqlgen::select_from<Book>().order_by(sqlgen::col("pages").desc()).to<std::vector<Book>>();
  return conn->read(query);
}
}  // namespace select_from_read_to_vector
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_department_employees {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string department_name;
  std::string location;
};

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
  double salary;
};

struct JoinResult {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::optional<std::string> first_name;
  std::optional<std::string> last_name;
  std::optional<double> salary;
};

struct JoinAnalysis {
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
};

JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_conn("test_db.sqlite");

  // INNER JOIN
  auto inner_join = conn->selectFrom<Department>("d")
      .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
      .join<Employee>("e", sqlgen::JoinType::inner, "d.department_id = e.department_id")
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<JoinResult> results;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          JoinResult jr;
          jr.department_name = res.get<std::string>("d.department_name");
          jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
          jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
          jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
          results.push_back(jr);
        }
        return results;
      });

  // LEFT JOIN
  auto left_join = conn->selectFrom<Employee>("e")
      .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
      .left_join<Department>("d", "e.department_id = d.department_id")
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<JoinResult> results;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          JoinResult jr;
          jr.department_name = res.get<std::string>("d.department_name");
          jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
          jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
          jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
          results.push_back(jr);
        }
        return results;
      });

  // RIGHT JOIN
  auto right_join = conn->selectFrom<Department>("d")
      .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
      .right_join<Employee>("e", "d.department_id = e.department_id")
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<JoinResult> results;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          JoinResult jr;
          jr.department_name = res.get<std::string>("d.department_name");
          jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
          jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
          jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
          results.push_back(jr);
        }
        return results;
      });

  // FULL JOIN (emulated with UNION)
  // Since SQLite doesn't support FULL JOIN, we need to combine LEFT and RIGHT joins
  auto full_join_left = conn->selectFrom<Employee>("e")
      .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
      .left_join<Department>("d", "e.department_id = d.department_id");

  auto full_join_right = conn->selectFrom<Department>("d")
      .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
      .right_join<Employee>("e", "d.department_id = e.department_id")
      .where("e.employee_id IS NULL");  // Exclude duplicates from right join

  auto full_join = conn->selectFrom(full_join_left.as("left").union_(full_join_right.as("right")))
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<JoinResult> results;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          JoinResult jr;
          jr.department_name = res.get<std::string>("d.department_name");
          jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
          jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
          jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
          results.push_back(jr);
        }
        return results;
      });

  JoinAnalysis analysis;
  analysis.inner_join_results = inner_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());
  analysis.left_join_results = left_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());
  analysis.right_join_results = right_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());
  analysis.full_join_results = full_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());

  return analysis;
}

}  // namespace analyze_department_employees

          jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
          jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
          jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
          results.push_back(jr);
        }
        return results;
      });

  // LEFT JOIN
  auto left_join = conn->selectFrom<Employee>("e")
      .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
      .left_join<Department>("d", "e.department_id = d.department_id")
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<JoinResult> results;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          JoinResult jr;
          jr.department_name = res.get<std::string>("d.department_name");
          jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
          jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
          jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
          results.push_back(jr);
        }
        return results;
      });

  // RIGHT JOIN
  auto right_join = conn->selectFrom<Department>("d")
      .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
      .right_join<Employee>("e", "d.department_id = e.department_id")
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<JoinResult> results;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          JoinResult jr;
          jr.department_name = res.get<std::string>("d.department_name");
          jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
          jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
          jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
          results.push_back(jr);
        }
        return results;
      });

  // FULL JOIN (emulated)
  auto full_join = conn->selectFrom(
      conn->selectFrom<Employee>("e")
          .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
          .left_join<Department>("d", "e.department_id = d.department_id")
          .union(
              conn->selectFrom<Department>("d")
                  .fields("d.department_name", "e.first_name", "e.last_name", "e.salary")
                  .right_join<Employee>("e", "d.department_id = e.department_id")
                  .where("e.employee_id IS NULL"))  // Exclude duplicates
          .transform([](auto range) {
            std::vector<JoinResult> results;
            for (auto& res : range) {
              JoinResult jr;
              jr.department_name = res.get<std::string>("d.department_name");
              jr.first_name = res.get<std::string>("e.first_name");
              jr.last_name = res.get<std::string>("e.last_name");
              jr.salary = res.get<double>("e.salary");
              results.push_back(jr);
            }
            return results;
          }));

  JoinAnalysis analysis;
  analysis.inner_join_results = inner_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());
  analysis.left_join_results = left_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());
  analysis.right_join_results = right_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());
  analysis.full_join_results = full_join.value_or(std::vector<JoinResult>());

  return analysis;
}

}  // namespace analyze_department_employees

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace concat_function {

struct Person {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
};

struct FullName {
  std::string full_name;
};

inline auto concat_function() {
    return sqlgen::select_from<Person>()
        .fields(sqlgen::expr("CONCAT(first_name, ' ', last_name)").as("full_name"))
        .to_sql();
}

}  // namespace concat_function
```
</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace aggregation_arithmetic {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  double price;
};

 */
inline std::string aggregation_arithmetic() {
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>({sqlgen::avg(Product::price) + 10.0});
    return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace aggregation_arithmetic
```

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <optional>

namespace coalesce_function {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::optional<std::string> nickname;
};

struct NicknameResult {
  std::string display_name;
};

inline auto coalesce_function() {
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Profile>()
        .select(sqlgen::coalesce(Profile::nickname, "Anonymous").as("display_name"));
    return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace coalesce_function
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace create_index_basic {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string title;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates creating an index.
 */
inline auto create_index_basic() {
    return sqlgen::create_index<Book, sqlgen::Column<std::string, "title">>(
               "idx_book_title"
           ).str();
}

}  // namespace create_index_basic

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <vector>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"

namespace get_top_products {

std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count) {
  const auto query =
      "SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category, SUM(p.price * s.quantity) AS total_sales "
      "FROM Product p "
      "INNER JOIN Sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id "
      "GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category "
      "ORDER BY total_sales DESC "
      "LIMIT ?";

  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto res = conn->execute(
        query,
        sqlgen::values(static_cast<int>(limit_count)));
    return sqlgen::internal::collect::vector(
        res | transform([](auto&& _row) -> TopProduct {
          return rfl::from_named_tuple<TopProduct>(_row.values());
        }));
  } catch (std::exception& e) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to retrieve top products: " + std::string(e.what()));
  }
}

}  // namespace get_top_products
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int stock;
};

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn) return false;

  auto txn = begin_transaction(conn);
  if (!txn) return false;

  for (const auto& [pid, qty] : received) {
    const auto query = update<Product>()
        .set(Product::stock = Product::stock + qty)
        .where(Product::product_id == pid);
    auto res = execute(*txn, query);
    if (!res) {
      rollback(*txn);
      return false;
    }
  }

  return commit(*txn);
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;

};

  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    
    const std::string create_view_sql =
        "CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS "
        "SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS total "
        "FROM Order o "
        "INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
        "GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer;";
    conn->execute(create_view_sql);
    
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<OrderSummary>();
    auto results = conn->template read<std::vector<OrderSummary>>(query);
    
    return results;
}
}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "weekday_sales_analysis.h"

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto session = conn->make_session();

  const auto sales = session->table<Sales>();

  const auto query = sales.select(
      sales.sale_time.strftime("%w").cast<int>().as("weekday"),
      sales.quantity.sum().as("total_quantity")
  ).group_by("weekday").order_by("weekday");

  const auto result = session->execute(query);

  std::vector<WeekdayStats> stats;
  for (const auto& row : result) {
    stats.emplace_back(
        .weekday = row["weekday"].as<int>(),
        .total_quantity = row["total_quantity"].as<int>());
  }

  return stats;
}

}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace update_with_arithmetic {

struct Player {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int score;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates updating a column with arithmetic.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string update_with_arithmetic() {
  auto session = sqlgen::make_sqlite_session(":memory:");
  auto query = session->update<Player>().set(Player{.score = Player::score + 10});
  return session->to_sql(query);
}

}  // namespace update_with_arithmetic
```
</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <string>

namespace project_time_tracking {

// Structs as defined...

std::vector<TimeMetrics> project_time_tracking() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = R"(
    WITH CTE AS (
      SELECT 
        p.project_id,
        p.name,
        t.start_time,
        t.end_time,
        julianday(t.end_time) - julianday(t.start_time) AS duration_days,
        strftime('%W', t.start_time) AS week
      FROM Project p
      INNER JOIN TimeLog t ON p.project_id = t.project_id
    ),
    GroupedCTE AS (
      SELECT 
        project_id,
        name,
        week,
        SUM(duration_days) AS total_duration_days
      FROM CTE
      GROUP BY project_id, week
    ),
    RankedCTE AS (
      SELECT *,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY project_id, week ORDER BY start_time) AS rn
      FROM CTE
    )
    SELECT 
      g.project_id,
      g.name,
      strftime('%d', r.start_time) AS day,
      strftime('%m', r.start_time) AS month,
      strftime('%Y', r.start_time) AS year,
      strftime('%H', r.start_time) AS hour,
      strftime('%M', r.start_time) AS minute,
      strftime('%S', r.start_time) AS second,
      g.total_duration_days AS duration_days
    FROM GroupedCTE g
    INNER JOIN RankedCTE r ON g.project_id = r.project_id AND g.week = r.week
    WHERE r.rn = 1;
  )";

  const auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<TimeMetrics> metrics;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    TimeMetrics tm;
    tm.project_id = row.get<int>("project_id");
    tm.name = row.get<std::string>("name");
    tm.day = row.get<int>("day");
    tm.month = row.get<int>("month");
    tm.year = row.get<int>("year");
    tm.hour = row.get<int>("hour");
    tm.minute = row.get<int>("minute");
    tm.second = row.get<int>("second");
    tm.duration_days = row.get<double>("duration_days");
    metrics.push_back(std::move(tm));
  }

  return metrics;
}

}  // namespace project_time_tracking

#endif
